ety: Prk. सण्मिच्छरो; S. शनैश्र्चरः or शनैस्+चरः
def: s.m. (lit. 'slow-going'), The planet Saturn; — Saturday; (met.) ill-luck; poverty, penury; dirt; dirty clothes; a dirty, ill-looking fellow; a miser; a glutton
more: n. m. S. शनैश्चर 1. Saturn. Iskī āṅkhoṅ meṅ sanīchar hai . 2. Saturday (Black Monday). 1. Āj jāne kaisā sanīchar āyā thā ki ab-tak chain nahīṅ milā . 2. Jummā khel khilāve, sanīchar pakaṛ bulāve . Native School boys. Each Friday shows such sport and play, to school taken off on Saturday. 3. Penury; poverty. Jumma chhoṛ sanīchar nahāë uskā sanīchar kabhī na jāë . Prov. Who Friday skips and bathes on Saturday, from him hard Poverty will never keep away. (Friday is the day appointed in the Hadis for Mahommedans to bathe in). 4. A dirty, ill-looking fellow. 5. Dirt; dirty clothes. Āj kapṛe badle, sanīchar utār ḍālā . 6. A miser. 7. A great eater; a glutton. sanīchar ānā , v. n. To come under the influence of Saturn or one's evil star. Tumhāre sir sāṛhe-sāt baras kā sanīchar āyā hai .
subs: (kisī-ke sir), The evil influence of Saturn to come upon (the head of anyone); to come under the influence of an evil star, to suffer misfortune or ill-luck.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi سنیچر (sanīcar) (c. 1695), from Prakrit 𑀲𑀡𑀺𑀘𑁆𑀘𑀭 (saṇiccara), likely from Sanskrit *शनैश्चर (*śanaiścara), from शनि (śani) + चर (cara). Cognate with Awadhi and Bhojpuri सनीचर (sanīcar), Punjabi ਛਨਿੱਛਰ /چَھنِچَّھر (chanicchar), Sindhi ڇَن٘ڇَرُ (chañcharu).

Jo tū sāṅjhe bhāī, bahan bulāë, tere se kuchh nāhīṅ māṅgtī, Māṅgūṅ tīyal pachās, āṅgī oṛhne deṛh sau, Anat banat ke bichhve lā! ghūṅgrū, banat kā joṛā lā! Joṛe banat kā ghoṛā lā! ghoṛe banat kā chābuk lā! Itnā de to bahan bulā, nāhīṅ apne ghar bhalī . Hin. Girls' song. If you, my brother Sānjha, should your sister call, I nothing ask of you but fifty suits in all, Of wraps, and boddices seven score and ten or so, Fine lace and handsome rings, and bells too for my toe, A horse fit for my spouse, smart whip on horse to lay, All these give me, then for your sister send you may, If not, in mine own house as well I 'd rather stay. 2. Mixing together, intermixture, blending, confusing, confounding, confusion; irregular mixture, unlawful intermarriage, mixture of caste; a mixed caste or race, degraded caste (proceeding from the union of a man with a woman of a higher caste, or from the promiscuous intercourse of the four tribes, and again from the indiscriminate cohabitation of their descendants amongst each other. Most Hindūs of the present age are of one of the many branches of this race, the highest of which is impure, and is inferior to the Śūdra. According to the Rāmāyan, under the term saṅkar two kinds of men are included, 1˚ yonisaṅkar, 'mixed or degraded by birth'; 2˚ āćār-saṅkar, 'degraded by conduct').

def: adj. S. सम्मुख Present; before one's face (آگے 1, 2, 3.). 1. Nā pūchhā unse 'kaun ho tum,' na apne jī kī bāt kahī, Na karnā kuchh inkār paṛā, na kahnā ṭhairā yoṅ hī sahī, Jab chhoṛī khuāhish bose kī, phir kāheko dushnām sahī, Jab sanmukh ho gaë chanchal ke sab chhor havas yeh bāt rahī, Ṭuk dekh liyā, dil shād kīyā, khush-waqt hue aur chal nikle . Pure love. Nazīr. I asked not 'Maid, who are you, say,' nor did my heart betray, I'd naught to say, could she say nay? so be it as it may, The wish to kiss her cast away, why hear abuse foul, pray? When come before that sprightly fay all lust subdued, each day One look, my heart made glad, I blessed my stars, and took my way. 2. Shām ghaṭā ek nār kahāve, Sukh ke samaë voh kām na dve; Dukh ke samaë voh san-mukh hove, Aisī nār na dekhī hove . Riddle. Ans. Ḍhāl shield. Making perfect, perfecting, completing, finishing, polishing, refining, perfection, refinement; institution; education; accomplishment; conception, idea, notion; impression, design; form, mould; the power of memory; self-reproductive quality; faculty, capacity, instinct, habit, passion; operation, influence; decoration, embellishment, ornament, elegance; making sacred, hallowing, purification, consecration, dedication; purity; a sanctifying or purificatory rite or essential ceremony; investiture with the sacred thread; obsequies; a marriage-ceremony.
more: n. m. 1. Share ( ans , 1.); lot. Uskā itnā hī sanskār thā . Prov. Saying. The share that fate decreed him he hath received. 2. An essential purificatory rite among the Hindus; purification; consecration. 3. Regeneration.
s. m. An essential and purificatory rite among the Hindūs; purification, consecration.

def: part. & s.m. Carefully or accurately formed, artificially made, or produced, or constructed, artificial, elaborated, highly wrought, refined, polished, perfected, completed, finished; decorated, ornamented, embellished; excellent, best; cleansed, purified; consecrated, hallowed, sanctified; initiated; married; — language formed by accurate grammatical rules; refined, or polished, or highly-wrought speech; the sacred and classical language of the Hindūs
more: n. f. The Sanskrit language. It is esteemed a perfect language, formed by infallible rules, and the language of the gods.
subs: (˚ta+anu˚), adj. & adv. Sanskritized; having relation to the Sanskrit language; in accordance with the Sanskrit; after the manner of the Sanskrit.
